mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening, depressing quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL correlate with positive 60-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization that drives corporate earnings revisions and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent fails to generate positive 20-day returns during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 1.4 million coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in more than half of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth above 200 thousand and unemployment stability below 4.0 percent confirms labor market resilience that supports consumer spending and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS growth and low UNRATE fail to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation persisting above 3.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 4.0 percent creates negative real rates that erode purchasing power and depress quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.0 percent combined with FEDFUNDS below 4.0 percent correlates with positive 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized reflects broad economic expansion that directly lifts corporate revenues and supports positive equity market performance over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent fails to generate positive 20-day returns in walk-forward validation.
